key_insight: Verification is treated as a new scaling axis for LLMs, and LLM-as-a-Verifier turns judgment into continuous, fine-grained scores instead of discrete labels.
engineering_relevance: This gives practitioners a drop-in way to rank agent outputs, monitor task progress, and provide denser feedback for improving agentic systems and RL training.
method: Compute the expectation over scoring-token logits to produce continuous verification scores.
Scale verification along three axes: score granularity, repeated evaluation, and criteria decomposition.
Use a cost-efficient ranking algorithm to select the best candidate from multiple solutions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports state-of-the-art results on multiple benchmarks, including Terminal-Bench V2 (86.5%), SWE-Bench Verified (78.2%), RoboRewardBench (87.4%), and MedAgentBench (73.3%).
watch_item: Performance may depend on careful prompt design and calibration of scoring tokens, so deployment should validate robustness across tasks and model variants.
